title: Unified resonant-manifold framework for dynamical quantum phase transitions
authors: Jesse J. Osborne, Cheuk Yiu Wong, Jad C. Halimeh
abstract: Dynamical quantum phase transitions (DQPTs) are an exciting paradigm of out-of-equilibrium criticality in many-body systems manifested in nonanalytic behavior in the return rate to the initial state following a sudden quench. While previous work has tried to distinguish between distinct types of DQPTs, such as regular and anomalous, or manifold and branch, a comprehensive understanding of why each type appears in a given scenario is still lacking. In this work, we propose a unified framework addressing this gap in terms of the energy structure of different product state configurations. In particular, while manifold DQPTs are governed by resonances within the initial state manifold, branch DQPTs are governed by resonances with a transitional manifold of states dynamically connected to the initial manifold by low-order processes. We show that the (ir)regularity of branch DQPTs is related to the multiplicity of this transitional manifold, and we also observe exotic periods of extended degeneracy in the return rate (beyond the conventional level crossing of a DQPT) which are also conditioned on the structure of this transitional manifold. We demonstrate this by studying quenches of two different configurations in the 1 + 1D Z_2 LGT to various parameter regimes. Our findings provide a dynamical mechanism underlying branch DQPTs and frames DQPTs as probes of resonant connectivity in constrained Hilbert spaces, paving the way to a more complete understanding of the multifaceted nature of dynamical criticality.
summary: This paper introduces a new framework to unify different types of dynamical quantum phase transitions by focusing on energy resonances between state manifolds. By studying a 1+1D lattice gauge theory, the authors show that the nature of these transitions depends on the multiplicity and symmetry of transitional states. This provides a way to use DQPTs as probes for the resonant connectivity of constrained quantum systems.
main_problem: The lack of a comprehensive understanding of why different types of dynamical quantum phase transitions (DQPTs), such as regular vs. anomalous and manifold vs. branch, occur in specific many-body scenarios.
main_result: The authors propose a unified resonant-manifold framework where manifold DQPTs are driven by resonances within the initial state manifold, while branch DQPTs are driven by resonances with a transitional manifold of states.
method: The study uses infinite Matrix Product State (iMPS) simulations with the Time-Dependent Variational Principle (TDVP) and spectral analysis of energy resonances.
model_or_system: A 1+1D Z2 Lattice Gauge Theory (LGT) featuring dynamical matter fields, including terms for particle hopping (J), staggered fermion mass (mu), and external electric field strength (h).
key_observables: Fidelity (return rate to the initial state), electric flux, and particle number difference.
important_parameters: Fermionic mass (mu), electric field strength (h), hopping strength (J), and a bias term (delta).
main_assumptions: The study focuses on the mass-resonance regime and assumes the dynamics can be captured by the connectivity of product-state manifolds in a constrained Hilbert space.
figures_summary: Figure 1 maps the parameter space of the Z2 LGT; Figure 4 shows the evolution of return rates and local observables for different electric field strengths; Figure 8 displays quench dynamics for fully polarized and staggered-link states.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of DQPT classification, proposes the resonant-manifold framework, applies it to the Z2 LGT model, analyzes specific regimes (mass-resonance, large-h, and U(1) limit), and discusses the implications for understanding resonant connectivity.
why_interesting: It provides a fundamental mechanism for classifying non-equilibrium phase transitions based on energy structure and connectivity, which is highly relevant for understanding many-body dynamics and out-of-equilibrium criticality.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. The parameter space of the Z2 LGT (1), emphasiz- ing the regimes characterized by the emergence of branch (B) and manifold (M) DQPTs for quenches of the fully-polarized (fp) and staggered-link (sl) initial states. We consider four parameter regimes: (a) µ = h = 0, the effective free-fermion limit, (b) the zero-mass regime µ = 0, (c) the mass-resonance regime µ = h, and (d) the infinite-mass limit µ = h →∞. As we approach (d), the dynamics of the Z2 LGT becomes that of the spin-1/2 U(1) QLM, quenching from the charge- proliferated (CP) and vacuum (vac) initial states (the positive vacuum is shown in the diagram). Black (white) circles repre- sent occupied (empty) matter sites [and...

FIG. 2. The evolution of the return rate (top) and particle number difference (bottom) of fully polarized/staggered-link state quenched to µ = h = 0 in the Z2 (1). At this point, the model is equivalent to the free-fermion model (4).

FIG. 3. A schematic energy diagram of the different product- state configurations considered in the Z2 LGT, which forms the central organizing principle of this work. At µ = h = 0, all configurations are degenerate in energy, while adding a finite h separates the energies of the fully polarized states from the staggered-link states. In the mass-resonance regime µ = h, one of the fully polarized states is brought into resonance with the two staggered-link configurations, while the other remains energetically detuned.

FIG. 4. The evolution of the return rate (top panels) and electric flux and particle number difference (bottom panels) following the quench of the (a,c) fully polarized and (b,d) staggered-link initial states [illustrated in the upper right corner of the upper panel in (a) and (b), respectively] in the Z2 LGT to µ = 0 and (a,b) h = 0.5J and (c,d) h = J, obtained using infinite matrix product state numerics. FIG. 5. The evolution of the return rate (top panels) and electric flux and particle number difference (bottom panels) following the quench of the (a,c) fully polarized and (b,d) staggered-link initial states [illustrated in the upper right corner of the upper panel in (a) and (b), respectively] in the Z2 LGT at mass resonance to (a,b) µ = h = 0.75J and (c,d) µ = h = 2J.
